title: WOS Policy Parameter Config
description: A WOS Policy Parameter Config sidecar document. Provides date-indexed parameter values for temporal parameter resolution in WOS workflows. Government workflows apply rules effective at specific dates, not today's date. Each parameter declares its effective-date schedule and a resolution date reference -- the case state field that determines which date-effective value applies. Follows the OpenFisca model of date-indexed parameter values.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosPolicyParameters.description: Document type marker identifying this as a WOS Policy Parameter Config sidecar document (Governance S13). MUST be the string '1.0'. Processors use this field to dispatch to the policy parameter resolver; its absence means the document is not a recognized Policy Parameter Config.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for WOS Policy Parameter Config sidecar

targetWorkflow.description: URI of the WOS Kernel Document these policy parameters apply to. Must match the Kernel Document's url field exactly. Processors MUST reject a Policy Parameter Config whose targetWorkflow does not match the url of the Kernel Document being loaded (Governance S13.2).

x-lm.intent: Binds this sidecar to its parent kernel workflow

version.description: Semantic version of this Policy Parameter Config document. Useful for audit trails — allows workflows to pin a specific parameter set for reproducibility.

title.description: Human-readable name for this parameter configuration. Displayed in tooling and governance dashboards.

description.description: Human-readable description of this parameter set's purpose and coverage. Intended for authors and auditors.
examples: Date-indexed eligibility thresholds and benefit rates for the benefits adjudication workflow.
Annual procurement thresholds and deadline parameters for purchase-order approval workflows.

parameters.description: Named parameters with date-indexed values. Parameter names become available in the evaluation context under 'parameters.[name]'. Each parameter resolves to the value in effect on the case's resolution date (Governance S13.3).

eligibilityThreshold.description: Maximum household income for program eligibility

appealDeadlineDays.description: Number of days to file an appeal after adverse decision notice

x-lm.intent: Date-indexed parameters that resolve to the correct value for each case based on its relevant dates

bindings.description: Named regulatory version bindings with date-indexed document references. Binding names become available in the evaluation context under 'parameters.[name]', resolved to the effective URI for the case's resolution date. Ensures the case uses the form or service that was in effect at the time of the case event, not today (Governance S13.4).

eligibilityForm.description: Formspec Definition for the eligibility determination form

incomeVerificationService.description: Decision service endpoint for automated income verification

x-lm.intent: Date-indexed document bindings that resolve to the correct versioned URI for each case based on its relevant dates

description.description: Human-readable description of what this parameter represents and how it is used in eligibility or decision logic.
examples: Maximum household income for program eligibility
Number of days to file an appeal after adverse decision notice

type.description: JSON value type of this parameter's values. Determines how the resolved value is typed in the evaluation context.

unit.description: Unit of measure for this parameter's values, used in tooling and documentation only (not enforced by the processor).

resolutionDateRef.description: Case state field path that supplies the resolution date for this parameter. The processor looks up the most recent values entry on or before this date (Governance S13.3). Different parameters within the same document MAY use different resolution dates — e.g., eligibility thresholds resolve against applicationDate while appeal deadlines resolve against adverseDecisionDate.

x-lm.intent: Determines WHICH date is used to look up this parameter's effective value -- different parameters can use different dates

authority.description: Regulatory or statutory authority establishing this parameter's existence and legal basis. Used for audit documentation.

values.description: Date-indexed values ordered by effective date. Each entry is effective from its date until the next entry's date.

x-lm.intent: The temporal schedule of values -- resolution picks the most recent entry on or before the resolution date

effectiveDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which this value becomes effective. The resolver picks the entry with the most recent effectiveDate that is on or before the resolution date.

value.description: The parameter value effective from this date until the next entry's effectiveDate. Type is unconstrained here but MUST match the ParameterDefinition.type declared on the parent parameter.

authority.description: Specific regulatory or statutory authority for this individual value change. Used in audit traces and explanation generation.

id.description: Unique binding identifier. Becomes the key in the evaluation context under 'parameters.[id]'.

description.description: Human-readable description of what versioned document or service this binding controls. Used in tooling and audit documentation.
examples: Formspec Definition for the eligibility determination form
Decision service endpoint for income verification

resolutionDateRef.description: Case state field path that supplies the resolution date for this binding. Uses the same mechanism as ParameterDefinition.resolutionDateRef: the resolver picks the most recent values entry on or before this date (Governance S13.4). Different bindings MAY use different resolution dates within the same document.

x-lm.intent: Determines WHICH date is used to look up this binding's effective document version

bindingType.description: Type of document being versioned. 'formspec': Formspec Definition (Core S1.4). 'jsonSchema': JSON Schema document. 'service': service endpoint URI. 'mapping': Formspec Mapping DSL document. Processors MAY apply type-specific validation but MUST NOT alter the resolution mechanism based on bindingType.

x-lm.intent: Declares what kind of versioned document the binding URI references

authority.description: Regulatory or statutory authority establishing this binding's existence and legal basis. Used in audit documentation and explanation generation.

values.description: Date-indexed document references ordered by effective date. Each entry is effective from its date until the next entry's date.

x-lm.intent: The temporal schedule of document versions -- resolution picks the most recent entry on or before the resolution date

effectiveDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which this document version becomes the binding reference. The resolver picks the most recent entry on or before the resolution date.

value.description: URI of the versioned document or service endpoint that is the binding reference from this effectiveDate until the next entry's effectiveDate.

authority.description: Specific regulatory or statutory authority for this individual version change. Used in audit traces and explanation generation.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
